channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Legal Expert Questions Trump's Description of Strike Against Alleged Tren de Aragua Leader
item.description: A legal expert has raised concerns about President Donald Trump's announcement of a U.S. military operation that reportedly killed the leader of Tren de Aragua, a Venezuelan criminal organization. Tru...

item.title: Commentator Criticizes Reported Iran Proposal, Calling It Politically Damaging for Trump
item.description: A Raw Story article highlights comments made by Michael Weiss, an editor at Insider, regarding reported details of a potential agreement between the United States and Iran. According to information at...

item.title: Japan Successfully Launches the First H3-30 Rocket Variant on Test Mission Carrying Six Small Satellites
item.description: Japan’s space agency, JAXA, successfully launched the eighth H3 rocket on June 12, 2026, in the Flight 6 (F6) mission from the Yoshinobu Launch Complex at the Tanegashima Space Center. The mission was...

item.title: Texas GOP convention criticized after live elephant incident draws mockery and animal welfare concerns
item.description: At a recent Texas Republican Party convention, an unusual promotional moment involving a live elephant intended to support Governor Greg Abbott's re-election campaign sparked widespread attention and ...

item.title: AJK protesters regroup at sit-in sites after overnight dispersal claims
item.description: In Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK), supporters and activists of the proscribed Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) regrouped in large numbers at protest sites in Rawalakot after initially dispersing over...

item.title: Strategist Predicts Major Changes at CNN Following DOJ Approval of Paramount-Skydance Warner Bros. Acquisition
item.description: Political strategist Chai Komanduri argued during an appearance on MS NOW that CNN could face significant editorial and personnel changes following the U.S. Department of Justice's approval of Paramou...

item.title: Canberra teenage footballers selected for AFL national talent pathway tournament
item.description: The article is part of The Canberra Times' live Canberra sports coverage for Saturday, 13 June 2026. The main focus is on two young Australian Rules football players from Canberra who have attracted t...

item.title: Ben Domenech warns Republicans about potential Senate vulnerabilities ahead of midterms
item.description: Ben Domenech, opinion editor at the right-leaning Daily Wire and husband of Meghan McCain, offered a cautious assessment of Republican prospects in upcoming U.S. Senate races during an interview with ...

item.title: Study finds alcohol may increase cravings for savoury ultra-processed foods through protein appetite mechanisms
item.description: A new Australian study has explored why people often crave savoury snacks such as chips, nachos and salted nuts when drinking alcohol. Researchers found that alcohol may stimulate a biological appetit...

item.title: GOP aides criticize White House strategy and leadership approach ahead of midterms
item.description: A June 2026 report highlights growing frustration among Republican congressional aides toward the Trump White House as the U.S. midterm elections approach. According to reporting cited from the Daily ...

item.title: World Cup opening revives scrutiny of FIFA’s long history of political associations
item.description: This opinion and analysis article argues that controversies surrounding FIFA’s claim of being an apolitical organisation extend far beyond its recent relationship with US President Donald Trump. Publi...

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Photos Capture Natalie Wood During the Filming of Gypsy in 1962
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday highlights a collection of behind-the-scenes photographs of actress Natalie Wood taken during the production of the musical film 'Gypsy' in 1962. At the time, Wood w...

item.title: Four Taiwanese Men Detained for Immigration Violations After Yacht Grounding in Manila Bay
item.description: Four Taiwanese nationals were arrested after authorities discovered that they were illegally staying in the Philippines following a maritime incident in Manila Bay. The men were initially rescued by t...

item.title: Starmer defends defence spending strategy amid ministerial resignations and leadership pressure
item.description: The Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has defended his leadership and decisions on defence spending following the resignation of two senior defence ministers and growing political pressure within his pa...

item.title: Journalist Reports Iranian Negotiators Used Psychologists to Shape Messages During U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: A report discussed by investigative journalist Jeremy Scahill claims that Iranian negotiators involved in diplomatic discussions with the United States have consulted senior psychologists to help eval...

item.title: Report: Trump Allies Explore Use of Bank Settlement Funds to Compensate Jan. 6 Defendants
item.description: According to reporting cited in the article, allies of President Donald Trump have discussed a potential financial mechanism that could redirect money from ongoing Justice Department investigations in...

item.title: Veteran '60 Minutes' Producer Michael Gavshon Announces Departure Following Disputes Over Editorial Decisions
item.description: Michael Gavshon, a longtime producer at CBS News and the television program '60 Minutes,' announced that he will leave the show at the end of June after more than three decades with the program and ov...

item.title: Low Oil Inventories at Cushing, Oklahoma Raise Concerns About U.S. Supply Stability
item.description: A report highlighted growing concerns about declining oil inventories at Cushing, Oklahoma, one of the most important oil storage and distribution hubs in the United States. Often referred to as the '...

item.title: Uber Driver Lawsuit Alleges Kiefer Sutherland Assaulted and Threatened Him During Los Angeles Ride
item.description: A lawsuit filed by Uber driver Rafael Manvelyan has brought forward new allegations against actor Kiefer Sutherland regarding an incident that allegedly occurred in Los Angeles on January 12. Accordin...

item.title: India lodges diplomatic protest with US after three Indian mariners are killed in Gulf shipping incidents
item.description: India has formally conveyed its strong objection to the United States following incidents in the Gulf region that resulted in the deaths of three Indian mariners. External Affairs Minister S. Jaishank...
